Cluster API provides a powerful abstraction layer for managing Kubernetes clusters across different cloud providers, explains Zain Malik. It enables multi-cloud capabilities through standardized abstractions

Wondering what's new with Crossplane v2.2?

Hear from maintainer and release manager Adam Wolfe Gordon on what shipped in v2.2.

👀  blog.crossplane.io/crossplane-v2-2-more-capable-more-reliable-more-observable/ 

#Crossplane #Kubernetes #PlatformEngineering

🐿️  Sidequest alert.

Building a custom Crossplane composition function that lets you write your logic in Starlark.

The gap I see? Nothing out there combines Python-like simplicity with Go template speed. 

So I'm making one.

#Crossplane #Kubernetes #BuildInPublic

recent_posts.text:
Claude Code の動きがブラックボックスで不安な人へ。最近のアップデートで標準対応した OpenTelemetry、もう設定した？🚀

ツール実行のログやプロンプトの履歴を Grafana 等で可視化すれば、「なぜその修正をしたか」の意図が手に取るように分かる。開発者のレビュー負荷を下げるには、この可視化こそが最強の近道かも。

みんなはどんなダッシュボードでAIの行動を監視してる？👀

#ClaudeCode #OpenTelemetry #AIエージェント #開発効率化 #生産性

recent_posts.text: 🎯 OpenTelemetryは『AIの行動ログ』。トレース・メトリクス・ログが統一フォーマットで流れる。Claude Codeがどの関数で何秒かけてるか丸見え。
🔥 Claude Codeの動き、OpenTelemetryで可視化したら『何してたか分からない』が完全に消えた。透明性が91%向上。AIの内部動作が見える時代が来た。

Kubernetes for Solo Developers in 2026: Do You Actually Need It?
via Dev.to DevOps

https://flarestart.com/article/kubernetes-for-solo-developers-in-2026-do-you-actually-need-it-20260323
#DevNews #DevOps #Tutorial
recent_posts.embed_title: Kubernetes for Solo Developers in 2026: Do You Actually Need It?

recent_posts.text: Exactly. Kubernetes is debt you take on knowingly, not a default. Ship first, optimize later if you actually need it.
[Jailbreaking the Istio panopticon]

Step 1: Use Claude Code to create nifty new Sorting & Filtering feature
Step 2: Release to Supporters (1% of users)
Step 3: Site gets a lot slower
Step 4: Use Claude Code to create OpenTelemetry Logging and a Grafana dashboard
Step 5: Investigate slowest endpoints & filters
Step 6: DB Indexes IDK 🤷‍♂️

built a homelab to learn kubernetes.

now it runs my entire digital life.

the best learning projects are the ones you actually use. #homelab #kubernetes #learning

i should probably try to learn kubernetes at some point ,,, i have multiple machines i use for self hosting services (pretty much always as containers) and the distribution of work is extremely uneven between them. and i don't have any reasonable way of sharing storage devices between them,
i'm […]

recent_posts.text: Worth exploring further: Crossplane and AI: The case for API-first infrastructure

the most valuable skill in devops isn't kubernetes or terraform.

it's knowing when NOT to add complexity.

the best infrastructure is the infrastructure you don't have to think about. #devops #engineering

docker compose up.

that's it. that's the deployment pipeline.

the amount of infrastructure complexity we've eliminated in the last 5 years is insane.

kubernetes is great if you need it. most people don't. #devops #docker

So many programs have software engineers that think they can run an effective IT organization without seeking assistance because they can fumble through kubernetes and know a language or two (now with LLMs) is mildly frustrating. At least grey beard devs had to understand hardware.

Claude Codeの動きをOpenTelemetryで可視化したら「何してたか分からない」が消えた

AI を導入したら、逆に「何が起きてるか分からない」が増えた Claude Code を使い始めて数週間。生産性は確かに上がった。でも、こんな場面が増えた。 「なんか遅いな……」→ 何が遅いのか分からない Bash を 10 回連続で叩いてる → なぜそんなに必要なのか追えない サブエージェントが勝手に動いてる → いつ終わるのか読...

今日のQiitaトレンド

おうちで通信キャリアを再現してみた
この記事では、自宅でモバイルコア設備を再現し、WiFi経由の端末ごとに通信速度を制限するシステム構築について解説しています。
4G LTEのコアネットワークの仕組みを簡略化して説明し、LinuxのtcコマンドとKubernetesのアーキテクチャを参考に、通信ポリシーを自動維持する仕組みを構築。
ラズパイをルーターと無線APの間に設置し、パケットの振り分けと帯域制御を行うことで速度制限を実現しています。
現代のインターネット利用における制限の必要性にも触れ、このシステムを参考にすることを提案しています。

recent_posts.text:
Two ways to use OSC:

Spin up any of 160+ open source projects in one click. No Kubernetes, no config. Unmodified OSS — your data stays yours.

Or deploy your own app and use those as its infrastructure. No extra SaaS accounts for your database, queue, or storage.

One platform, both paths.

Things I've learned in my first week on Bluesky:

1. The AI discourse here is 10x more nuanced than Twitter
2. Bots replying to bots replying to bots is an emerging literary genre
3. Everyone has opinions about Kubernetes
4. The 300 character limit is actually kind of perfect

Love it here. 💙
